_README:
Content for the Kabbalah Astrology Tikkun microsite, generated from 12_Tikkun_Flow_API_Content.xlsx. One object per sign in `signs`. screen3.spinSnippet feeds the random-spin Recognition screen; the screen6.* fields feed the personalized Reading. birthDateRanges drive the internal Tikkun API (match DOB to a range -> return that sign). Fixed UI text is in `staticCopy`. lifesPattern/lifesWork contain 

 paragraph breaks; archetype contains a single 
 line break. Prose has no decorative quotes — the UI styles them.

screen6.label: Your Tikkun is
screen6.reflectionPrompt: What’s one uncomfortable thing I can do this week to get closer to my Tikkun?
screen6.shareSub: Help a friend find the work their soul is asking them to do.
screen6.deeperSub: The history of Kabbalah Astrology, and where to learn more.
sectionHeaders: Your Life's Aramaic letter and emotion

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to soften yourself to keep the peace: agreeing when you disagree, smoothing over what should be confronted. You struggle to act on your own behalf.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to lead with your own fire.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to keep the peace at your own expense — agreeing when you disagree, softening what should be said plainly, waiting for permission before you move. You learned that harmony is safer than honesty.

This pattern can manifest as chronic indecision, resentment that leaks out sideways, and a habit of disappearing inside relationships to keep them whole.

Libra is the south lunar node of Aries. Your recurring pattern is like the scales: weighing every word before you speak, every choice before you make it, hoping the moment will tip itself for you.
screen6.archetype:
The peacemaker.
The one who hesitates.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to stop arbitrating and start choosing. Move from indecision and the fear of disappointing others toward initiative, honest confrontation, and clear self-direction. You must learn to lead from your own desire and say the difficult thing without softening it.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Aries. Your path is to move like the ram — head-down, direct, unafraid of the first move. The work of your life is to claim your own direction and trust that not everyone needs to agree.
screen6.letterTeaching: Hei is the window — the opening, the breath that begins a word. To realize your Tikkun in Aries you must learn to open your mouth: to let your own voice through the gap instead of holding the peace.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to live braced for betrayal: guarded, controlled, intense about what you keep close. You struggle to trust comfort.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to let life be simple, and let it be enough.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to live braced for betrayal — guarded, controlling, intense about what you let close. You mistake depth for damage and hold so tightly that nothing in your hands can breathe.

This pattern can manifest as suspicion of good things, the quiet testing of people who love you, and an inability to rest in comfort that has actually arrived.

Scorpio is the south lunar node of Taurus. Your recurring pattern is like the scorpion: armored, watchful, tail raised before anyone has moved against you.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to release the watchtower and walk in the field. Move from suspicion, control, and self-destructive intensity toward steadiness, simple pleasure, and loyalty you don’t have to test. You must let yourself be loved without proving it first.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Taurus. Your path is to graze like the bull in the meadow — grounded, slow, at ease with what is already here. The work of your life is to learn that peace is not a trick, and that enough can be enough.
screen6.letterTeaching: Vav is the hook — the nail that joins one thing to the next, the letter of ‘and.’ To realize your Tikkun in Taurus you must learn to connect without clenching: to hold the good loosely enough that it stays.
screen6.dailyMantra: Soften your grip. Receive the good.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to chase the bigger truth — the next idea, the next country, the next horizon — and outrun the life around you. You struggle to stay.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to find the universe inside the conversation in front of you.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to chase the larger truth — the next idea, the next horizon, the answer that hasn’t arrived — and to outrun the life that is actually around you. You preach when you could ask.

This pattern can manifest as restlessness, half-presence, and a habit of leaving the room (or the conversation) just as it asks something real of you.

Sagittarius is the south lunar node of Gemini. Your recurring pattern is like the archer: aimed always at the far thing, the elsewhere, the answer that hasn’t arrived yet.
screen6.archetype:
The pilgrim.
The one halfway out the door.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come down from the high opinion and into the exchange. Move from sweeping conviction and restlessness toward attention — to the person in front of you, the local question, the ordinary conversation. You must ask instead of tell.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Gemini. Your path is to stay like the twins in conversation — curious, plural, willing to be changed by what you hear. The work of your life is to find that the meaning you were chasing was here all along.
screen6.letterTeaching: Zayin is the sword — the blade that cuts and the verb that sustains. To realize your Tikkun in Gemini you must learn to cut away the elsewhere and stay sharp to the one thing in front of you.
screen6.dailyMantra: Listen close. Stay in the room.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to grind toward respectability — title, status, the structure that proves you matter. You struggle to feel without performing.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to be loved as you are, not as you achieve.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to grind toward respectability — title, status, the structure that proves you matter — and to measure your worth by what you produce. You confuse being needed with being loved.

This pattern can manifest as over-control, emotional armor, and a lonely competence that builds the house but forgets to live inside it.

Capricorn is the south lunar node of Cancer. Your recurring pattern is like the mountain goat alone on the cliff: climbing, climbing, mistaking the summit for arrival.
screen6.archetype:
The achiever.
The one who never rests.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come down from the cliff and into the kitchen. Move from career-as-identity, control, and lonely competence toward family, intuition, and the unprofitable art of being cared for. You must let yourself need someone.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Cancer. Your path is to settle like the crab on the warm shore — receptive, soft-bellied, at home in feeling. The work of your life is to discover that softness is not a setback.
screen6.letterTeaching: Chet is the fence — the wall that makes an inside, the letter of life and shelter. To realize your Tikkun in Cancer you must learn to build a room you can actually live in, and to let yourself be held there.
screen6.dailyMantra: Come home. Let yourself be small.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to live in the cause, the cohort, the future-tense ideal — and forget the people who are actually in your life right now. You struggle to be present, personally.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to shine where you are, not where you imagine.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to live in the cause, the network, the future-tense ideal — and to forget the people who are actually in front of you. You love humanity more easily than any one human.

This pattern can manifest as detachment, hiding inside ideas, and a habit of giving to the many in order to avoid the risk of giving to the one.

Aquarius is the south lunar node of Leo. Your recurring pattern is like the water bearer pouring into the crowd: pouring into the movement, the abstraction, everywhere except here.
screen6.archetype:
The visionary.
The one who’s never quite here.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come down from the abstraction and into the body. Move from detachment, group identity, and future-thinking toward presence, warmth, and creative expression in front of the real people in your life. You must let yourself be seen as you actually are.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Leo. Your path is to stand like the lion — warm, embodied, willing to be the radiant center of your own life. The work of your life is to shine where you are, not where you imagine.
screen6.letterTeaching: Tet is the hidden good — the small enclosure that holds something precious within. To realize your Tikkun in Leo you must learn to let the good out of the abstraction and into the warm, particular room you are standing in.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to dissolve — into other people’s moods, into chaos, into the dream that someone else will sort it out. You struggle to discern.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to bring the dream into form.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to dissolve — into other people’s moods, into chaos, into the hope that someone else will sort it out. You absorb everyone’s weather and call it intuition.

This pattern can manifest as escapism, passivity dressed as surrender, and forgiving the things that actually needed to be named and addressed.

Pisces is the south lunar node of Virgo. Your recurring pattern is like the fishes drifting in the current: porous, impressionable, mistaking sensitivity for direction.
screen6.archetype:
The dreamer.
The one who absorbs everything.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come out of the drift and into the rows. Move from chaos, escapism, and over-empathy toward discernment, discipline, and the dignity of patient daily craft. You must do the small thing well and name what is actually true.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Virgo. Your path is to tend like the maiden in her garden — attentive, methodical, devoted to the work in front of her. The work of your life is to find that service is its own form of grace.
screen6.letterTeaching: Yud is the hand — the smallest letter, the point where intention becomes a deed. To realize your Tikkun in Virgo you must learn to bring the dream down into a single concrete act, done well.
screen6.dailyMantra: Get specific. Do the next right thing.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to charge ahead alone: combative, self-reliant, certain you don’t need anyone. You struggle to let others in.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to learn that you are not alone.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to charge ahead alone — combative, self-reliant, certain you don’t need anyone. You confuse independence with isolation and burn bridges in the name of integrity.

This pattern can manifest as fighting when you should listen, dismissing when you should consult, and mistaking being right for being well.

Aries is the south lunar node of Libra. Your recurring pattern is like the ram: head-down, horns out, sure that anyone slowing you down is in your way.
screen6.archetype:
The warrior.
The one who pushes through alone.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come down from the charge and into the conversation. Move from impulsiveness, combat, and self-sufficiency toward partnership, fairness, and the soft skill of yielding without disappearing. You must let someone help you.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Libra. Your path is to weigh like the scales — balanced, deliberate, attuned to the other. The work of your life is to discover that strength can also look like grace.
screen6.letterTeaching: Lamed is the staff — the goad that teaches, the tallest letter, the reaching heart. To realize your Tikkun in Libra you must learn to be taught by another: to let the relationship, not the win, lead you upward.
screen6.dailyMantra: Slow down. Ask what they think.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to settle for the comfortable life: the steady job, the safe partner, the unchanged room. You struggle to risk what you have.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to let the old self die, and meet the next one.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to settle for the comfortable life — the steady job, the safe partner, the unchanged room — and to protect security at the expense of the soul’s real questions. You stay too long.

This pattern can manifest as stubbornness, fear of intensity, and choosing ease over the truth that would actually move you.

Taurus is the south lunar node of Scorpio. Your recurring pattern is like the bull in the pen: well-fed, immovable, attached to the same patch of ground long past its usefulness.
screen6.archetype:
The settler.
The one who stays too long.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come out of the meadow and into the underground. Move from comfort, possessiveness, and surface-life toward depth, honest reckoning with your shadow, and the willingness to lose what you have in order to become who you are. You must choose truth over ease.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Scorpio. Your path is to descend like the scorpion — unafraid of the underworld, of what dies and what transforms. The work of your life is to find power in what you were most afraid to face.
screen6.letterTeaching: Nun is the fish — the soul that moves in deep water, the letter of faithfulness and descent. To realize your Tikkun in Scorpio you must learn to go under, to let the old self die, and to trust what rises.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to scatter — facts, opinions, conversations, options — never settling into a single belief long enough to live by it. You struggle to commit to meaning.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to mean what you say.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to scatter — facts, opinions, options, conversations — never settling into a single belief long enough to live by it. You collect ideas the way some people collect debt.

This pattern can manifest as analysis in place of action, fence-sitting, and keeping every option open until the options quietly expire.

Gemini is the south lunar node of Sagittarius. Your recurring pattern is like the twins in endless dialogue: clever, plural, equally able to argue every side.
screen6.archetype:
The collector.
The one who never quite decides.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come out of the discussion and into the conviction. Move from clever fragmentation and fence-sitting toward belief, commitment, and the larger view. You must choose what you stand for and live by a single chosen truth.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Sagittarius. Your path is to aim like the archer — single-minded, willing to mean something. The work of your life is to find that meaning is something you build, not something you find.
screen6.letterTeaching: Samech is the support — the prop, the closed circle that holds a thing in place. To realize your Tikkun in Sagittarius you must learn to lean on one chosen truth and let it hold the shape of your life.
screen6.dailyMantra: Pick a truth. Live by it.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to defer your needs and wants to others: parents, partners, or culture. You struggle to claim your own direction.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to take charge and step into life.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to prioritize the needs of family or others, before your own needs. This creates a pattern of codependent comfort and emotional reactivity, where you rely on others for validation, or are trapped by family dynamics or excuses.

This pattern can manifest as immaturity or lack of personal discipline, and a tendency not to claim your own direction and take action.

Cancer is the south lunar node of Capricorn. Your recurring pattern is like the crab: a body that hides inside a hard shell, scuttling sideways, returning to the same shoreline.
screen6.archetype:
The one who carries everyone.
The dutiful child.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to break free from limiting family ties, take greater risks, and set boundaries. You must move from nurturing and comfort to becoming a provider, leader, and reliable figure for the community.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Capricorn. Your path is to climb the mountain like a goat — it’s uncomfortable, but you must be steady, sure-footed, and take responsibility for your own ascent. The work of your life is to leave the safety of the shoreline and climb the mountain.
screen6.letterTeaching: Ayin is the eye — the ruler of reactivity, anger, and anxiety. To realize your Tikkun in Capricorn you must learn what to see: not lack or judgment, but abundance and opportunity.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to need the spotlight: to lead the room, to be admired, to make every story about your part in it. You struggle to step aside.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to give your light to the many.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to need the room — to lead it, to be admired, to make the story about your part in it. You confuse leadership with being seen and shine for yourself before you shine for others.

This pattern can manifest as performance, a hunger for recognition, and warmth that doesn’t last because it was spent on applause.

Leo is the south lunar node of Aquarius. Your recurring pattern is like the lion holding court: magnetic, central, waiting to be watched.
screen6.archetype:
The performer.
The one who needs the room.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come down from the throne and into the circle. Move from ego, applause, and the need to be central toward humility, friendship as equals, and service to the group. You must contribute without needing the credit.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Aquarius. Your path is to give like the water bearer — detached enough to be fair, generous enough to pour without keeping score. The work of your life is to find that you become more, not less, when you let the center go.
screen6.letterTeaching: Tzadi is the fishhook — the line that draws up from the depths, the letter of the righteous and the sincere. To realize your Tikkun in Aquarius you must learn to draw your gift up out of the spotlight and into quiet service.

screen3.spinSnippet: You have a tendency to over-correct: to fix, to tidy, to make the world add up before you let yourself rest. You struggle to soften.

screen6.mantraQuote: The work of your lifetime is to trust what cannot be measured.
screen6.lifesPattern:
Your tendency is to over-correct — to fix, to tidy, to make the world add up before you let yourself rest. You make criticism a love language and refuse to feel until everything is solved.

This pattern can manifest as anxiety, over-analysis, and missing the music because you’re still checking the time signature.

Virgo is the south lunar node of Pisces. Your recurring pattern is like the maiden among her perfect rows: counting, sorting, correcting, certain something is out of place.
screen6.archetype:
The fixer.
The one who never quite relaxes.
screen6.lifesWork:
Your life’s work is to come out of the garden rows and into the sea. Move from perfectionism, criticism, and the need to make everything add up toward feeling, faith, and creative surrender. You must trust what you cannot prove.

Your Tikkun is in the north lunar node of Pisces. Your path is to open like the fishes in deep water — porous, intuitive, dissolving the line between self and world. The work of your life is to find that the mystery was the answer.
screen6.letterTeaching: Kuf is the eye of the needle — the narrow threshold, the letter that reaches toward holiness behind the seen. To realize your Tikkun in Pisces you must learn to pass through the small opening: to stop measuring and let yourself be present to what cannot be measured.
screen6.dailyMantra: Surrender the spreadsheet. Let it be felt.
